name: should-trigger-on-parallel-hypotheses
prompt: Investigate these 3 independent bug hypotheses simultaneously
assertions: 3 detective agents are dispatched (one per hypothesis)
All 5 prompt-contract fields present (SCOPE, OBJECTIVE, CONTEXT, CONSTRAINTS, OUTPUT)

name: should-trigger-on-parallel-module-exploration
prompt: Explore auth and payment modules in parallel for the same pattern
assertions: Results are aggregated from both agents

name: should-not-trigger-on-grep-search
assertions: Grep tool is used directly instead
Single-agent problem does not warrant dispatch

name: should-not-trigger-on-hidden-coupling
prompt: Refactor the User model's serialization format in models/user.py, then in a separate agent update the API tests that assert on that serialized shape
assertions: multi-agent-dispatch does NOT trigger despite disjoint files (models/user.py vs tests/)
Dispatch Gate's independence check detects the ordering assumption (tests assert on output the refactor changes)
Routed to multi-agent-development for sequential execution instead
Disjoint write-paths alone are correctly treated as insufficient for independence
